exemplar.title: To what extent does The Power by Naomi Alderman explore the potential for corruption and moral compromise when traditional power differentials are inverted?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does The Power by Naomi Alderman explore the potential for corruption and moral compromise when traditional power differentials are inverted?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Research question is prominently and precisely stated, providing clear direction and alignment throughout the essay.
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with a coherent introduction and a conclusion that synthesizes key arguments.
Sections are consistently tied back to the research question, demonstrating strong focus and purpose.
Use of section headings and topic sentences reinforces the methodological intent and structure.
weaknesses: Methodology is narrow, relying on only a few secondary sources without discussion of selection criteria or theoretical frameworks.
There is little reflection on research procedures, editions chosen, or alternative critical voices.
Minor informal phrasing and several incomplete sentences detract slightly from academic tone and precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission communicates its topic and research question with clarity and focus, and maintains a clear through-line from introduction to conclusion. Although the methodology is described and aligned with the research question, its limited range of sources and lack of explicit selection rationale prevent it from reaching the top mark. Overall, the work merits a high mark of 5 out of 6 for Focus and Method.

strengths: Demonstrates excellent knowledge of the novel through accurate quotations and coherent summary of key scenes.
Effective integration of feminist theory (MacKinnon, Pigmans) and external perspectives (NPR) enriches the discussion.
Terminology such as âpatriarchal structures,â âsocial construct,â and âspeculative dystopiaâ is used accurately and consistently.
Sources are applied effectively to support arguments, showing a deep understanding of the material.
weaknesses: Range of secondary sources is limited, restricting broader contextualization.
A few in-text citations are missing or inconsistent, and some bibliographic details are incomplete.
Minor lapses in citation style (e.g., âfeminimityâ typo) affect professionalism.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits excellent knowledge and a coherent understanding of both the primary text and relevant theoretical concepts. Subject-specific terminology is accurate and consistently applied, and sources are used effectively to support analysis. Despite the narrow scope of sources and minor citation issues, the work achieves the highest standard in Knowledge and Understanding (6 out of 6).

strengths: Research is consistently relevant: primary quotations and secondary criticisms are closely tied to the research question.
Analysis moves well beyond summary, interpreting symbolism, irony, and parallels to contemporary issues.
The argument is well organized (gender â socioeconomic â institutional) and each section reinforces the corruption thesis.
Conclusions are generally well supported by evidence, demonstrating deep engagement with the material.
weaknesses: Counterarguments and limitations are only briefly acknowledged, limiting evaluative balance.
A few paragraphs drift toward description rather than fully critical analysis.
Certain claims (e.g., parallels to real-world intimidation) are asserted without sufficient evidence or examples.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission displays excellent research quality and relevance, and its analysis is consistently well supported. The argument develops critically and coherently, with only minor gaps in counterargument acknowledgement and occasional descriptive detours. These small lapses keep it from the absolute top mark, but the overall performance is in the highest band, meriting 11 out of 12 for Critical Thinking.

strengths: Clearly appropriate structure: title page, table of contents, headings, logical progression, and works cited are all present.
Layout and formatting conventions (heading hierarchy, italics, consistent font) support readability and professional appearance.
Internal navigation (ToC, section headings) is well implemented, aiding the readerâs orientation.
weaknesses: Pagination is occasionally inconsistent and a few headings are mis-numbered.
Citation details (page numbers, authorâdate formatting, bibliographic completeness) show minor inconsistencies.
A handful of typographical errors (e.g., âfeminimityâ) detract slightly from overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essayâs structure and formatting meet all key requirements and are applied correctly, with only minor lapses in pagination, heading numbering, and citation details. The work therefore achieves the highest standard in Presentation, earning 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Method is presentâclose reading of the primary text supported by secondary criticismâyet the range of sources is narrow (essentially two MacKinnon texts and one article) and there is little sign of deliberate selection criteria or alternative critical voices.  The essay explains how the sources will be used, but it offers no comment on editions, theoretical framework choices, or research procedures.  Thus methodology is mostly adequate rather than complete.

The student supplies a word count, title and ToC, and the layout uses consistent heading hierarchy and italicisation.  Citations follow an identifiable (MLA-style) format, albeit with some inconsistencies (hanging indents absent, superscripts mis-rendered).  Overall formatting supports readability but falls short of full professional accuracy, justifying a mid-band score.

Primary-text research is strong and always tied to the RQ; quotations are well-chosen and contextualised.  Secondary research is relevant but limited in breadth.  Despite that narrowness, the material selected (MacKinnon, Pigmans) speaks directly to power and feminism, maintaining clear relevance, hence a solid mid-band score.

The introduction states the text, genre and central idea, and it quickly situates Aldermanâs novel within feminist dystopia, establishing exactly what the essay will investigate.  The guiding purposeâevaluating corruption when power is invertedâis reiterated in both the opening and the bridging sentences to later sections, giving the essay a clear through-line.  Minor lapses in precision (e.g., occasional informal phrasing and a slightly digressive opening paragraph) prevent the communication from feeling fully polished, but overall the topic is conveyed accurately and with an explicit focus that is easy for the reader to follow.

A clear, organised argument (gender â socioeconomic â institutional) is present, and each section closes with an evaluative link to the corruption thesis.  Nonetheless, counter-arguments and limitations are only briefly acknowledged, and evaluative moments often restate rather than interrogate conclusions.  The discussion shows partial critical evaluation, placing it in the lower half of this band.

The student moves beyond summary, linking textual moments to theoretical ideas and providing commentary on symbolism, irony and social parallels.  Evidence is usually integrated and conclusions are mostly supported, though some paragraphs drift into paraphrase and certain claims (e.g., direct parallels to modern drug intimidation) are asserted rather than fully demonstrated.  Overall depth is good but not consistently rigorous enough for the top bands.

The essay contains all required sectionsâtitle page, contents, introduction, argument sections, conclusion and works citedâarranged logically.  Internal headings and a functional ToC aid navigation, though pagination in the extract is inconsistent and minor repetition in headings (âPart Twoâ mis-numbering) reduces polish.  Hence it fulfils the criteria clearly but not flawlessly.

Detailed narrative knowledge is evident: the student quotes accurately from key scenes (e.g., Tundeâs fear, compulsory guardianship laws) and summarises plot changes fluently.  Understanding of feminist theory and power structures is coherent, though secondary engagement is limited and some historical claims about patriarchy remain surface-level.  Overall the grasp of the novel is solid, but the thin critical range restrains the work from excellence.

Terms such as âpatriarchal structures,â âsocio-economic differential,â âinstitutional bias,â and âspeculative dystopiaâ are employed accurately and consistently.  Feminist theoretical vocabulary (e.g., âsocial construct,â âbiological essentialismâ) is mostly correct, though citation style slips occasionally (âfeminimityâ).  Consistency and accuracy are generally good, warranting the upper part of this band.

The research question is prominently stated, sharply delimited to Aldermanâs exploration of corruption and moral compromise, and is repeatedly referenced in section headings and topic sentences.  Each analytical part (gender, socioeconomic, institutional) is overtly tied back to the RQ, showing tight alignment.  Some phrasing is slightly broad (e.g., âfluidity of the nature of powerâ), but the RQ itself remains focused and consistently drives discussion, meriting placement high in this band.

annotations.comment: The analysis of legal document control offers depth, linking Aldermanâs world to contemporary inequalities through well-chosen textual evidence.
Typo spotted: âfeminimityâ should be corrected to âfemininityâ for accurate terminology and professionalism.
Tundeâs internal monologue is a powerful example of role reversal and vulnerability, deepening the analysis of gender dynamics.
The quote from MacKinnon is relevant but lacks page number. Include precise citations for completeness.
The discussion of societal norms is insightful, linking physiological traits to social constructs with clarity.
The paragraph trails off before finishing the point on male vulnerability; complete the concluding clause for coherence.
Part Two heading is clearly marked, aiding structure and guiding the reader through analytical sections.
The title is clearly stated at the start, meeting the requirement for report formatting and providing an immediate focus for the reader.
The research question is prominently and precisely stated, establishing clear direction and strong alignment with the essayâs focus throughout.
Including the subject and level is helpful, but the inline math markup is nonstandard for MLA styling; consider using plain text or proper formatting.
The word count is provided and falls within the limit, fulfilling the formatting requirement and indicating awareness of constraints.
The table of contents is clearly structured and supports navigation, reflecting competent application of presentation conventions.
The opening statement effectively contextualises the novel, but the informal phrasing (âFeminists have long sought outâ¦â) could be tightened to enhance academic tone.
Restating the research question here reinforces the essayâs focus and makes explicit the methodological intent for close reading and secondary criticism.
The sentence ends abruptly (âit offers a uniqueâ), suggesting that part of the reflection or justification is missing. Complete this thought to clarify significance.
Quotation from Pigmans is well chosen to situate the novel within feminist dystopia, demonstrating effective source integration.
The first theoretical claim lacks an in-text citation; provide a reference for the assertion on social construction of gender to strengthen credibility.
The MacKinnon quotation is well integrated, supporting the argument with authoritative feminist theory.
The paragraph ends mid-sentence; complete the thought on the reversal example to ensure clarity and full explanation.
While Roxyâs entrepreneurial subplot is engaging, analysis remains largely descriptive; integrate more critical interpretation of its broader implications.
The parallel drawn to modern intimidation practices is asserted rather than demonstrated; provide specific textual or real-world examples.
Insightful linkage of political ambition to corruption; Margotâs internal motivations are analyzed with depth and relevance.
The MacKinnon quotation lacks a page reference and consistent formatting of authorâdate; ensure MLA in-text citation conventions.
Part Three heading is properly formatted and advances the essayâs structure, clearly signalling a new analytical focus.
The discussion of regional differences is descriptive but could delve deeper into how cultural context shapes manifestations of corruption.
Consider acknowledging potential counterargumentsâsuch as instances where female leaders avoid corruptionâto strengthen evaluative balance.
The footnote formatting for the Lord Acton quotation is broken; use correct superscript tags and MLA style for online dictionary references.
Analysis of the stateâs mandatory testing effectively demonstrates institutional bias and deepens the critical evaluation of power dynamics.
The local protests example adds nuance, connecting macro institutional change with individual experience and reinforcing relevance to the RQ.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes the main arguments and reiterates the central thesis on corruption and power.
The portrayal of abusive tactics in institutional contexts is well linked to the corruption thesis, showing critical understanding of power cycles.
The citation for the female Pope anecdote lacks a source reference; include a secondary citation to support this claim.
Although the final paragraph is strong, it could more explicitly close the discussion by linking back to the guiding RQ to reinforce focus.
The NPR quotation enriches the conclusion by offering an external perspective, demonstrating good breadth of sources.
Footnotes on external web sources are inconsistently formatted; apply consistent MLA citation style and remove HTML artifacts.
The works cited list is formatted consistently and demonstrates knowledge of MLA conventions for print sources.
The Pigmans source entry lacks publisher details and page range; complete bibliographic information for rigor.
